slides.title: Reusable Magic 🪄
slides.content:
A function is a reusable block of code that performs a specific task. 

Instead of copying and pasting the same 10 lines of code five times, you wrap them in a function and simply "call" the function 5 times.

slides.title: Function Syntax
slides.content: Use the `function` keyword, give it a name, add parentheses `()`, and curly braces `{}` for the body.

quizData.question: How do you correctly 'call' a function named `startGame` so that it runs?
quizData.explanation: You use the function name followed by parentheses to execute it.

slides.title: Receiving Inputs 📥
slides.content: Functions can receive data to work with. These are called **parameters** (or arguments), and they go inside the parentheses.

slides.title: Basic Parameters
slides.content: Here is a function that takes an input called `name`.
